content.text:
{
  "total_drawers": 8,
  "wings": {
    "project_alpha": 2,
    "strategy_convos": 3,
    "wing_team": 1,
    "wing_code": 1,
    "wing_user": 1
  },
  "rooms": {
    "backend": 1,
    "planning": 1,
    "technical": 3,
    "auth-migration": 2,
    "release-readiness": 1
  },
  "palace_path": "/tmp/mempalace-phase0-home-2zu7bmfb/.mempalace/palace",
  "protocol": "IMPORTANT — MemPalace Memory Protocol:
1. ON WAKE-UP: Call mempalace_status to load palace overview + AAAK spec.
2. BEFORE RESPONDING about any person, project, or past event: call mempalace_kg_query or mempalace_search FIRST. Never guess — verify.
3. IF UNSURE about a fact (name, gender, age, relationship): say "let me check" and query the palace. Wrong is worse than slow.
4. AFTER EACH SESSION: call mempalace_diary_write to record what happened, what you learned, what matters.
5. WHEN FACTS CHANGE: call mempalace_kg_invalidate on the old fact, mempalace_kg_add for the new one.

This protocol ensures the AI KNOWS before it speaks. Storage is not memory — but storage + this protocol = memory.",
  "aaak_dialect": "AAAK is a compressed memory dialect that MemPalace uses for efficient storage.
It is designed to be readable by both humans and LLMs without decoding.

FORMAT:
  ENTITIES: 3-letter uppercase codes. ALC=Alice, JOR=Jordan, RIL=Riley, MAX=Max, BEN=Ben.
  EMOTIONS: *action markers* before/during text. *warm*=joy, *fierce*=determined, *raw*=vulnerable, *bloom*=tenderness.
  STRUCTURE: Pipe-separated fields. FAM: family | PROJ: projects | ⚠: warnings/reminders.
  DATES: ISO format (2026-03-31). COUNTS: Nx = N mentions (e.g., 570x).
  IMPORTANCE: ★ to ★★★★★ (1-5 scale).
  HALLS: hall_facts, hall_events, hall_discoveries, hall_preferences, hall_advice.
  WINGS: wing_user, wing_agent, wing_team, wing_code, wing_myproject, wing_hardware, wing_ue5, wing_ai_research.
  ROOMS: Hyphenated slugs representing named ideas (e.g., chromadb-setup, gpu-pricing).

EXAMPLE:
  FAM: ALC→♡JOR | 2D(kids): RIL(18,sports) MAX(11,chess+swimming) | BEN(contributor)

Read AAAK naturally — expand codes mentally, treat *markers* as emotional context.
When WRITING AAAK: use entity codes, mark emotions, keep structure tight."
}

status_payload.aaak_dialect:
AAAK is a compressed memory dialect that MemPalace uses for efficient storage.
It is designed to be readable by both humans and LLMs without decoding.

FORMAT:
  ENTITIES: 3-letter uppercase codes. ALC=Alice, JOR=Jordan, RIL=Riley, MAX=Max, BEN=Ben.
  EMOTIONS: *action markers* before/during text. *warm*=joy, *fierce*=determined, *raw*=vulnerable, *bloom*=tenderness.
  STRUCTURE: Pipe-separated fields. FAM: family | PROJ: projects | ⚠: warnings/reminders.
  DATES: ISO format (2026-03-31). COUNTS: Nx = N mentions (e.g., 570x).
  IMPORTANCE: ★ to ★★★★★ (1-5 scale).
  HALLS: hall_facts, hall_events, hall_discoveries, hall_preferences, hall_advice.
  WINGS: wing_user, wing_agent, wing_team, wing_code, wing_myproject, wing_hardware, wing_ue5, wing_ai_research.
  ROOMS: Hyphenated slugs representing named ideas (e.g., chromadb-setup, gpu-pricing).

EXAMPLE:
  FAM: ALC→♡JOR | 2D(kids): RIL(18,sports) MAX(11,chess+swimming) | BEN(contributor)

Read AAAK naturally — expand codes mentally, treat *markers* as emotional context.
When WRITING AAAK: use entity codes, mark emotions, keep structure tight.
status_payload.protocol:
IMPORTANT — MemPalace Memory Protocol:
1. ON WAKE-UP: Call mempalace_status to load palace overview + AAAK spec.
2. BEFORE RESPONDING about any person, project, or past event: call mempalace_kg_query or mempalace_search FIRST. Never guess — verify.
3. IF UNSURE about a fact (name, gender, age, relationship): say "let me check" and query the palace. Wrong is worse than slow.
4. AFTER EACH SESSION: call mempalace_diary_write to record what happened, what you learned, what matters.
5. WHEN FACTS CHANGE: call mempalace_kg_invalidate on the old fact, mempalace_kg_add for the new one.

This protocol ensures the AI KNOWS before it speaks. Storage is not memory — but storage + this protocol = memory.

tools.description: Palace overview — total drawers, wing and room counts
tools.name: mempalace_status

tools.description: List all wings with drawer counts
tools.name: mempalace_list_wings

tools.description: List rooms within a wing (or all rooms if no wing given)
tools.name: mempalace_list_rooms

tools.description: Full taxonomy: wing → room → drawer count
tools.name: mempalace_get_taxonomy

tools.description: Get the AAAK dialect specification — the compressed memory format MemPalace uses. Call this if you need to read or write AAAK-compressed memories.
tools.name: mempalace_get_aaak_spec

tools.description: Query the knowledge graph for an entity's relationships. Returns typed facts with temporal validity. E.g. 'Max' → child_of Alice, loves chess, does swimming. Filter by date with as_of to see what was true at a point in time.

as_of.description: Date filter — only facts valid at this date (YYYY-MM-DD, optional)

tools.name: mempalace_kg_query

tools.description: Add a fact to the knowledge graph. Subject → predicate → object with optional time window. E.g. ('Max', 'started_school', 'Year 7', valid_from='2026-09-01').

source_closet.description: Closet ID where this fact appears (optional)

tools.name: mempalace_kg_add

tools.description: Mark a fact as no longer true. E.g. ankle injury resolved, job ended, moved house.

ended.description: When it stopped being true (YYYY-MM-DD, default: today)

tools.name: mempalace_kg_invalidate

tools.description: Chronological timeline of facts. Shows the story of an entity (or everything) in order.

entity.description: Entity to get timeline for (optional — omit for full timeline)

tools.name: mempalace_kg_timeline

tools.description: Knowledge graph overview: entities, triples, current vs expired facts, relationship types.
tools.name: mempalace_kg_stats

tools.description: Walk the palace graph from a room. Shows connected ideas across wings — the tunnels. Like following a thread through the palace: start at 'chromadb-setup' in wing_code, discover it connects to wing_myproject (planning) and wing_user (feelings about it).
tools.name: mempalace_traverse

tools.description: Find rooms that bridge two wings — the hallways connecting different domains. E.g. what topics connect wing_code to wing_team?
tools.name: mempalace_find_tunnels

tools.description: Palace graph overview: total rooms, tunnel connections, edges between wings.
tools.name: mempalace_graph_stats

tools.description: Semantic search. Returns verbatim drawer content with similarity scores.
tools.name: mempalace_search

tools.description: Check if content already exists in the palace before filing
tools.name: mempalace_check_duplicate

tools.description: File verbatim content into the palace. Checks for duplicates first.

content.description: Verbatim content to store — exact words, never summarized

tools.name: mempalace_add_drawer

tools.description: Delete a drawer by ID. Irreversible.

drawer_id.description: ID of the drawer to delete

tools.name: mempalace_delete_drawer

tools.description: Write to your personal agent diary in AAAK format. Your observations, thoughts, what you worked on, what matters. Each agent has their own diary with full history. Write in AAAK for compression — e.g. 'SESSION:2026-04-04|built.palace.graph+diary.tools|ALC.req:agent.diaries.in.aaak|★★★'. Use entity codes from the AAAK spec.

agent_name.description: Your name — each agent gets their own diary wing

entry.description: Your diary entry in AAAK format — compressed, entity-coded, emotion-marked

tools.name: mempalace_diary_write

tools.description: Read your recent diary entries (in AAAK). See what past versions of yourself recorded — your journal across sessions.

agent_name.description: Your name — each agent gets their own diary wing

last_n.description: Number of recent entries to read (default: 10)

tools.name: mempalace_diary_read
